fix_es: Bajo la ley federal, antes de rechazarte, el empleador debe darte una copia del reporte y tiempo para disputar. En Illinois, la ley prohíbe preguntas de antecedentes criminales en aplicaciones para empleadores con 15+ empleados.
action_es: Si te rechazan para un trabajo, pregunta: '¿Usaron verificación de antecedentes?' Si hay errores, dispútalos por escrito con la compañía de evaluación. Tienen 30 días. Guarda la carta de rechazo.

title: Your Rights Before They Shut Off Your Utilities
normal: Utility companies bill you monthly. When bills go unpaid, state rules require written notice, payment plan offers, and disconnection only as a last resort after a specific process.
broken: Millions of utility disconnections happen every year. Many people don't know the protections that already exist. Companies sometimes skip required steps — like offering payment plans or sending proper notice. In communities of color, shutoff rates are up to 4 times higher than in other areas, even when controlling for income.
fix: In Illinois, utilities cannot shut off heating December through March if you agree to a payment plan (Winter Weather Rule). They cannot disconnect when the forecast is at or below 32°F or above 95°F. A medical certificate from a doctor prevents shutoff for 60 days, renewable. Ten days of written notice is required before any disconnection. LIHEAP provides federal grants for utility bills regardless of immigration status. File complaints with the Illinois Commerce Commission at 1-800-524-0795.
action: If you received a shutoff notice, call the utility and say: 'I'd like to set up a payment plan.' Ask about LIHEAP assistance. If you or someone in your household has a medical condition, get a medical certificate from your doctor — it stops the shutoff for 60 days. Call CEDA at 773-571-1000 for Chicago-area LIHEAP.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Tus Derechos Antes de que Corten Tus Servicios Públicos
normal_es: Las compañías de servicios te facturan mensualmente. Las reglas estatales requieren aviso escrito, ofertas de plan de pago, y desconexión solo como último recurso.
broken_es: Millones de desconexiones ocurren cada año. Muchos no conocen las protecciones que existen. En comunidades de color, las tasas de corte son hasta 4 veces más altas.
fix_es: En Illinois, no pueden cortar calefacción de diciembre a marzo si aceptas un plan de pago. No pueden desconectar cuando el pronóstico es 32°F o menos o 95°F o más. Un certificado médico previene el corte por 60 días. LIHEAP da asistencia sin importar estatus migratorio. Quejas al 1-800-524-0795.
action_es: Si recibiste aviso de corte, llama y di: 'Quiero establecer un plan de pago.' Pregunta por LIHEAP. Si alguien tiene condición médica, obtén un certificado del doctor. Llama a CEDA al 773-571-1000.

title: The Registration Requirement That Blocks College Aid and Citizenship
normal: Federal law requires virtually all males aged 18 to 25 — including undocumented immigrants, refugees, and DACA recipients — to register with the Selective Service within 30 days of their 18th birthday. This is not military enlistment. It's a registration.
broken: Not registering blocks federal student financial aid, federal jobs, and federally funded job training. It can also delay or deny citizenship. After age 26, you can no longer register — the consequences become permanent unless you prove you didn't know about the requirement. Most immigrant men don't register because nobody told them.
fix: If under 26, register today at sss.gov or at any post office. If over 26, request a Status Information Letter at sss.gov and explain that your failure was not intentional — provide evidence of your immigration timeline and language barriers. For citizenship applicants over 31, the failure falls outside the 5-year good moral character window and generally doesn't block naturalization. Selective Service does NOT collect or share immigration status information.
action: If you're a male between 18 and 25, go to sss.gov right now and register. It takes 2 minutes. If you're over 26 and never registered, request a Status Information Letter and write a statement explaining why. Talk to an immigration attorney before your citizenship interview.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: El Requisito de Registro que Bloquea Ayuda Universitaria y Ciudadanía
normal_es: La ley federal requiere que virtualmente todos los hombres de 18 a 25 años — incluyendo indocumentados, refugiados y DACA — se registren en el Servicio Selectivo. No es alistamiento militar. Es un registro.
broken_es: No registrarse bloquea ayuda financiera federal, empleos federales y capacitación. Puede retrasar o negar la ciudadanía. Después de los 26, ya no puedes registrarte — las consecuencias son permanentes. La mayoría de hombres inmigrantes no se registran porque nadie les dijo.
fix_es: Si tienes menos de 26, regístrate hoy en sss.gov o en cualquier oficina de correos. Si tienes más de 26, solicita una Carta de Información de Estatus en sss.gov y explica que no sabías. El Servicio Selectivo NO recopila ni comparte información de estatus migratorio.
action_es: Si eres hombre entre 18 y 25, ve a sss.gov ahora y regístrate. Toma 2 minutos. Si tienes más de 26, solicita una Carta de Información de Estatus. Habla con un abogado de inmigración antes de tu entrevista de ciudadanía.

title: When Your Work Permit Expires Before the Renewal Arrives
normal: Employment Authorization Documents let eligible noncitizens work legally. You file a renewal up to 180 days before expiration. While it processes, you should be able to keep working.
broken: Over 2 million work permit applications are pending. Processing takes 2.5 to 12 or more months depending on category. A rule change in October 2025 eliminated automatic extensions for new applications — meaning if your renewal isn't processed in time, you lose work authorization even though you did everything right. Loss of work authorization cascades into job loss, health insurance loss, and housing instability.
fix: For applications filed before October 30, 2025, the 540-day automatic extension still applies for eligible categories. Show your employer the expired card plus your I-797C receipt notice. For delays, file an e-Request at egov.uscis.gov when processing exceeds posted times. Call USCIS at 1-800-375-5283. Your congressional representative's constituent services office can help expedite cases. For extended delays, mandamus lawsuits are increasingly common.
action: File your renewal as early as allowed — 180 days before expiration. Keep your I-797C receipt notice safe and give a copy to your employer. If processing exceeds posted times, file an e-Request AND contact your congressional representative's office. Say: 'My work permit renewal is delayed beyond normal processing times. I need a congressional inquiry.'
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cuando Tu Permiso de Trabajo Expira Antes de que Llegue la Renovación
noun_es: Brechas en Renovación de Permisos de Trabajo
normal_es: Los Documentos de Autorización de Empleo permiten trabajar legalmente. Presentas la renovación hasta 180 días antes del vencimiento.
broken_es: Más de 2 millones de solicitudes están pendientes. El procesamiento toma de 2.5 a 12+ meses. Un cambio de regla en octubre 2025 eliminó extensiones automáticas para nuevas solicitudes — pierdes autorización aunque hiciste todo bien.
fix_es: Para solicitudes antes del 30 de octubre de 2025, la extensión automática de 540 días aplica. Muestra a tu empleador la tarjeta vencida más tu recibo I-797C. Para demoras, presenta una solicitud electrónica en egov.uscis.gov. La oficina de tu representante en el Congreso puede ayudar a acelerar.
action_es: Presenta tu renovación lo antes posible — 180 días antes del vencimiento. Guarda tu recibo I-797C y dale copia a tu empleador. Si hay demora, presenta solicitud electrónica Y contacta la oficina de tu representante.
tool_label_es: Envía una Solicitud de Servicio a USCIS

title: How Wage Theft Works — and How to Get Your Money Back
normal: You work. Your employer pays you at least the legal minimum for every hour, including overtime at 1.5x after 40 hours per week. Your tips belong to you. You get a pay stub showing hours, rate, and deductions.
broken: Wage theft costs workers an estimated $50 billion per year — more than all robberies, burglaries, and auto thefts combined. Common forms include not paying overtime, paying below minimum wage, stealing tips, misclassifying employees as independent contractors, making people work off the clock, and illegal deductions. Low-wage workers, immigrants, and workers of color are targeted most often.
fix: Document everything — photos of your schedule, pay stubs, hours worked in a notebook. File a complaint with the DOL Wage and Hour Division at 1-866-487-9243 or your state labor department. In Illinois, call 312-793-2800. Complaints are free and confidential. The DOL investigates regardless of immigration status. Retaliation for filing is a separate federal violation.
action: If you think you're being underpaid, use the Worker Rights calculator on this site to check your state's minimum wage and calculate what you should be earning. If the math doesn't add up, call the DOL at 1-866-487-9243.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cómo Funciona el Robo de Salario — y Cómo Recuperar Tu Dinero
normal_es: Trabajas. Tu empleador te paga al menos el mínimo legal por cada hora, incluyendo tiempo extra a 1.5x después de 40 horas por semana. Tus propinas son tuyas. Recibes un recibo de pago.
broken_es: El robo de salario cuesta a los trabajadores aproximadamente $50 mil millones por año — más que todos los robos, asaltos y robos de auto combinados. Formas comunes incluyen no pagar tiempo extra, pagar menos del mínimo, robar propinas, clasificar incorrectamente a empleados como contratistas independientes, y hacer trabajar fuera del reloj.
fix_es: Documenta todo — fotos de tu horario, recibos de pago, horas trabajadas. Presenta una queja al DOL al 1-866-487-9243 o a tu departamento de trabajo estatal. En Illinois, llama al 312-793-2800. Las quejas son gratis y confidenciales. El DOL investiga sin importar el estatus migratorio.
action_es: Si crees que te están pagando menos, usa la calculadora de Derechos del Trabajador en este sitio para verificar el salario mínimo de tu estado. Si los números no cuadran, llama al DOL al 1-866-487-9243.

title: Your Child's Rights at School — IEPs, 504 Plans, and Discipline
normal: Schools must evaluate children who may need extra support, create plans with specific services, follow those plans in every classroom, communicate with parents in their language, and never ask about immigration status.
broken: Schools delay evaluations, write vague plans, don't share plans with teachers, pressure parents into signing, discipline students of color at three times the rate of white students, deny bilingual services, and sometimes discourage enrollment based on language or immigration status.
fix: Put everything in writing — email counts. Request evaluations in a letter. Attend every IEP or 504 meeting and bring someone you trust. If the school isn't following the plan, document it and file a complaint with the U.S. Department of Education Office for Civil Rights at ed.gov/ocr. In Illinois, call Equip for Equality at 800-537-2632 for free advocacy. For bilingual rights, contact MALDEF at 312-427-0701.
action: If your child is struggling at school, write this sentence and send it to the principal: 'I am requesting an evaluation for my child under IDEA and Section 504.' The school must respond.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Los Derechos de Tu Hijo en la Escuela — IEP, Plan 504 y Disciplina
normal_es: Las escuelas deben evaluar a niños que pueden necesitar apoyo extra, crear planes con servicios específicos, seguir esos planes, comunicarse con los padres en su idioma, y nunca preguntar sobre estatus migratorio.
broken_es: Las escuelas retrasan evaluaciones, escriben planes vagos, no comparten planes con maestros, presionan a padres para firmar, disciplinan a estudiantes de color al triple de la tasa de estudiantes blancos, y a veces desalientan la inscripción por idioma o estatus migratorio.
fix_es: Pon todo por escrito — el correo electrónico cuenta. Solicita evaluaciones en una carta. Asiste a cada reunión de IEP o 504 y lleva a alguien de confianza. Si la escuela no sigue el plan, documenta y presenta una queja ante la Oficina de Derechos Civiles del Departamento de Educación en ed.gov/ocr. En Illinois, llama a Equip for Equality al 800-537-2632.
action_es: Si tu hijo tiene dificultades en la escuela, escribe esta oración y envíala al director: 'Solicito una evaluación para mi hijo bajo IDEA y la Sección 504.' La escuela debe responder.

title: Going to Court — What to Expect and What to Bring
normal: Courts resolve disputes through a structured process. You get notice, you show up, both sides present evidence, a judge decides. The process is designed to be fair — even without a lawyer.
broken: Most people have never been to court. They don't know what to wear, where to go, what to say, or what their rights are. They miss deadlines that can't be undone. They don't show up and lose by default. They don't know they can negotiate before seeing the judge.
fix: Show up — that alone changes the outcome. Bring all your documents organized. Dress respectfully. Arrive early. If you can't afford a lawyer, ask about self-help centers at the courthouse, or call CARPLS at 312-738-9200 for free legal guidance. Use the Court Navigator on this site for step-by-step guidance for eviction, small claims, traffic, and family court.
action: If you have a court date coming up, open the Court Navigator and read the section for your type of case. Make a checklist of what to bring. If you need a lawyer, call Legal Aid Chicago at 312-341-1070. Typical costs: Small claims filing $50-150, service by sheriff $30-60. Eviction defense: free if you qualify for legal aid. Traffic: fine amount on ticket. Family court: varies, fee waivers available for low income.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Ir a la Corte — Qué Esperar y Qué Llevar
normal_es: Las cortes resuelven disputas a través de un proceso estructurado. Recibes aviso, te presentas, ambas partes presentan evidencia, un juez decide.
broken_es: La mayoría de la gente nunca ha ido a corte. No saben qué ponerse, adónde ir, qué decir, o cuáles son sus derechos. Pierden fechas límite. No se presentan y pierden por defecto.
fix_es: Preséntate — eso solo cambia el resultado. Lleva todos tus documentos organizados. Vístete con respeto. Llega temprano. Si no puedes pagar un abogado, pregunta sobre centros de autoayuda en el tribunal, o llama a CARPLS al 312-738-9200.
action_es: Si tienes una fecha de corte próxima, abre el Navegador de Corte y lee la sección para tu tipo de caso. Haz una lista de lo que debes llevar.

title: How Mortgage Lending Works — and Who Gets Denied
normal: You apply for a mortgage. The lender evaluates your income, credit, debt, and the property value. If you qualify, you get the loan. Federal law requires lenders to report every application including approvals, denials, and the applicant's race.
broken: Black applicants are denied mortgages at roughly twice the rate of white applicants nationally. Hispanic applicants face similar gaps. Even controlling for income, the disparity persists. Credit scoring models penalize communities with less generational wealth. Appraisal systems undervalue homes in Black and Latino neighborhoods. Lenders steer borrowers of color toward higher-cost products.
fix: If denied, the lender must give you a written reason. Compare denial rates by race in your county using the Lending Fairness tool — the data is public under HMDA. If you believe discrimination played a role, file a complaint with HUD at 1-800-669-9777 or the CFPB. Contact a HUD-approved housing counselor for free mortgage guidance at 1-800-569-4287.
action: Use the Lending Fairness tool to see denial rates by race in your county. If you were denied a mortgage, request the written adverse action notice and compare your profile to approval rates.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cómo Funcionan los Préstamos Hipotecarios — y Quién es Negado
normal_es: Solicitas una hipoteca. El prestamista evalúa tus ingresos, crédito, deuda y el valor de la propiedad. Si calificas, obtienes el préstamo. La ley federal requiere que los prestamistas reporten cada solicitud.
broken_es: Los solicitantes negros son negados hipotecas a aproximadamente el doble de la tasa de solicitantes blancos a nivel nacional. Los solicitantes hispanos enfrentan brechas similares. Los modelos de puntaje de crédito penalizan a comunidades con menos riqueza generacional.
fix_es: Si te niegan, el prestamista debe darte una razón por escrito. Compara las tasas de negación por raza en tu condado usando la herramienta de Equidad en Préstamos. Si crees que hubo discriminación, presenta una queja con HUD al 1-800-669-9777 o el CFPB.
action_es: Usa la herramienta de Equidad en Préstamos para ver las tasas de negación por raza en tu condado.

title: How to Check If Your Building Is Safe
normal: The city inspects buildings for safety — structure, fire exits, plumbing, electrical, heating, pest control. When violations are found, the owner gets a notice and deadline to fix them. You can report problems by calling 311.
broken: Some landlords ignore violations for years. Tenants live with mold, broken heating, roach infestations, and fire hazards without knowing the city already cited the building. Open violations mean known problems the owner hasn't fixed. Most tenants never check.
fix: Look up your building on the Building Safety tool — it pulls real violation data from the City of Chicago. If there are open violations, your landlord has a legal obligation to fix them. Under Chicago's RLTO, tenants can withhold rent for certain unfixed violations. Document everything with photos and dates. Report new problems at 311 or chicago.gov/311.
action: Enter your address in the Building Safety tool. If you see open violations, take photos, write down dates, and contact Metropolitan Tenants Organization at 773-292-4988.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cómo Verificar Si Tu Edificio es Seguro
normal_es: La ciudad inspecciona edificios por seguridad — estructura, salidas de incendio, plomería, electricidad, calefacción, control de plagas. Cuando se encuentran violaciones, el dueño recibe un aviso y fecha límite para arreglarlas.
broken_es: Algunos propietarios ignoran violaciones por años. Los inquilinos viven con moho, calefacción rota, plagas y riesgos de incendio sin saber que la ciudad ya citó al edificio.
fix_es: Busca tu edificio en la herramienta de Seguridad de Edificios — obtiene datos reales de violaciones de la Ciudad de Chicago. Si hay violaciones abiertas, tu propietario tiene obligación legal de arreglarlas.
action_es: Ingresa tu dirección en la herramienta de Seguridad de Edificios. Si ves violaciones abiertas, toma fotos, anota fechas, y contacta a Metropolitan Tenants Organization al 773-292-4988.

title: How to Find Every Benefit You Qualify For
normal: The federal government operates dozens of programs — SNAP, WIC, Medicaid, CHIP, EITC, LIHEAP, housing assistance, school meals. Each has income thresholds based on the Federal Poverty Level and household size. If you qualify, you're entitled to them.
broken: Over $60 billion in benefits goes unclaimed every year. Only 80% of eligible families get SNAP. Only 57% get WIC. Only 17% get heating help. Only 13% of eligible kids get childcare subsidies. The barriers are application complexity, language, and fear — not eligibility.
fix: Use the Benefit Screener on this site. Enter your household size and income. In 2 minutes you'll see which programs you likely qualify for, with direct links to apply. The screener is 100% private — nothing is saved or sent.
action: Open the Benefit Screener. Enter your household size and income. You may be leaving thousands of dollars on the table.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cómo Encontrar Todos los Beneficios Para los Que Calificas
normal_es: El gobierno federal opera docenas de programas — SNAP, WIC, Medicaid, CHIP, EITC, LIHEAP, asistencia de vivienda, comidas escolares. Cada uno tiene umbrales de ingresos basados en el Nivel Federal de Pobreza.
broken_es: Más de $60 mil millones en beneficios no se reclaman cada año. Solo 80% de las familias elegibles reciben SNAP. Solo 57% reciben WIC. Solo 17% reciben ayuda para calefacción.
fix_es: Usa el Evaluador de Beneficios en este sitio. Ingresa el tamaño de tu hogar e ingresos. En 2 minutos verás para qué programas probablemente calificas.
action_es: Abre el Evaluador de Beneficios. Ingresa el tamaño de tu hogar e ingresos. Puedes estar dejando miles de dólares sobre la mesa.

title: What's in Your Air and Water — and Who Put It There
normal: The EPA monitors air quality, tracks industrial pollution, inspects facilities for compliance, and enforces environmental laws. Communities can access this data publicly.
broken: In February 2025, the EPA removed EJScreen — the primary tool communities used to assess environmental hazards. 100+ Office of Environmental Justice employees were placed on leave. Air monitors aren't always placed in impacted communities. Facilities self-report much of their pollution data.
fix: The underlying data still exists. Use the Environmental Check on this site to find EPA-monitored facilities near you, see which ones have violations, and check air quality. If you find violations, file a complaint with your state EPA or the federal EPA regional office.
action: Enter your zip code in the Environmental Check. See what's in your air and who's polluting near you.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Qué Hay en Tu Aire y Agua — y Quién Lo Puso Ahí
normal_es: La EPA monitorea la calidad del aire, rastrea la contaminación industrial, inspecciona instalaciones y hace cumplir las leyes ambientales.
broken_es: En febrero 2025, la EPA removió EJScreen — la herramienta principal que las comunidades usaban para evaluar riesgos ambientales.
fix_es: Los datos subyacentes aún existen. Usa la Verificación Ambiental en este sitio para encontrar instalaciones monitoreadas por la EPA cerca de ti.
action_es: Ingresa tu código postal en la Verificación Ambiental. Mira qué hay en tu aire y quién contamina cerca de ti.

title: How to Track Whether the City Is Fixing Your Block
normal: You call 311. The city logs a service request. A crew is assigned. The pothole, streetlight, rat complaint, or tree trim gets resolved. The data is public.
broken: Requests sit open for weeks or months. Some are closed without action. Resolution descriptions are vague. Most residents don't know they can check the status. Neighborhoods with less political power get slower response times.
fix: Use the 311 Tracker on this site. Enter your address or zip code and see every service request on your block — type, date, status, days open. If requests are being ignored, bring the data to your alderman. Patterns are harder to dismiss than individual complaints.
action: Enter your address in the 311 Tracker. See what's been reported and what's been fixed — or hasn't.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cómo Rastrear Si la Ciudad Está Arreglando Tu Cuadra
normal_es: Llamas al 311. La ciudad registra una solicitud de servicio. Se asigna un equipo. El problema se resuelve. Los datos son públicos.
broken_es: Las solicitudes quedan abiertas por semanas o meses. Algunas se cierran sin acción. Los vecindarios con menos poder político reciben tiempos de respuesta más lentos.
fix_es: Usa el Rastreador 311. Ingresa tu dirección y mira cada solicitud de servicio en tu cuadra — tipo, fecha, estado, días abierta.
action_es: Ingresa tu dirección en el Rastreador 311. Mira qué se ha reportado y qué se ha arreglado — o no.

title: How to Read a Medical Bill Line by Line
normal: After a medical visit, you receive an Explanation of Benefits (EOB) from your insurer, then a bill from the provider. The EOB shows what was charged, what insurance paid, and what you owe. The bill should match.
broken: Half to 80% of medical bills contain errors — duplicate charges, unbundled codes, incorrect patient info, charges for services not received. Hospitals often send a summary bill that hides line items. Most people pay without checking. The billing system is designed to be unreadable.
fix: Always request the ITEMIZED bill — not the summary. Compare every line to your EOB. Look for: duplicate charges (same code twice), unbundling (one procedure split into parts to charge more), wrong dates, services you don't recognize. If you find errors, call the billing department and dispute in writing. If uninsured, ask about the hospital's financial assistance policy — nonprofit hospitals are required to have one.
action: Call your provider and say: 'I need the itemized bill with CPT codes, not the summary.' Compare it to your EOB line by line.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Cómo Leer una Factura Médica Línea por Línea
normal_es: Después de una visita médica, recibes una Explicación de Beneficios de tu aseguradora, luego una factura del proveedor.
broken_es: Del 50% al 80% de las facturas médicas contienen errores — cargos duplicados, códigos separados, información incorrecta del paciente.
fix_es: Siempre solicita la factura DETALLADA — no el resumen. Compara cada línea con tu EOB. Busca cargos duplicados, fechas incorrectas, servicios que no reconoces.
action_es: Llama a tu proveedor y di: 'Necesito la factura detallada con códigos CPT, no el resumen.' Compárala con tu EOB línea por línea.

title: The 30% Rule — Why Rent Keeps Trapping Families
normal: The federal standard says housing is 'affordable' when it costs 30% or less of household income. This threshold determines eligibility for housing assistance and how much subsidized rent you pay.
broken: Nearly half of all renters in America pay more than 30% of income on rent. One in four pays more than 50%. The 30% rule was set in 1981 and has never been adjusted. Meanwhile wages stagnated and rents doubled. A minimum wage worker cannot afford a 2-bedroom apartment anywhere in America at 30% of income. The 'affordable' standard defines the problem but doesn't solve it.
fix: If you're paying more than 50% of income on rent, apply for housing assistance now — waitlists are long (27 months average) but they move. Check if your area has rental assistance programs through your city or county. If your rent increased illegally (no proper notice, exceeds local limits), you may have legal recourse. Track your rent-to-income ratio and use it when applying for assistance — 'cost-burdened' status (>30%) strengthens applications.
action: Calculate: take your monthly rent, divide by your monthly gross income, multiply by 100. Over 30% = cost-burdened. Over 50% = severely cost-burdened. Use this number when applying for housing assistance.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: La Regla del 30% — Por Qué la Renta Sigue Atrapando a las Familias
normal_es: El estándar federal dice que la vivienda es 'asequible' cuando cuesta 30% o menos del ingreso del hogar.
broken_es: Casi la mitad de todos los inquilinos pagan más del 30% de sus ingresos en renta. Uno de cada cuatro paga más del 50%.
fix_es: Si pagas más del 50% en renta, solicita asistencia de vivienda ahora — las listas de espera son largas pero se mueven.
action_es: Calcula: toma tu renta mensual, divide por tu ingreso mensual bruto, multiplica por 100. Más de 30% = sobrecargado.

title: What Happens When You Don't File Taxes — and What You're Leaving Behind
normal: Every year you file a tax return reporting your income. The IRS calculates what you owe or what you're owed back. For low-income workers, the refund — especially the EITC — can be worth thousands of dollars.
broken: 5 million people eligible for the EITC don't file taxes at all, leaving $7 billion unclaimed. Many are afraid of the IRS, don't think they earn enough to file, or can't afford a preparer. Others file but don't claim credits they qualify for. Meanwhile, the IRS audits EITC recipients at 5.5 times the rate (ProPublica/IRS research, 2024) of other filers.
fix: If you earned any income — even part-time, even cash — file. Free options: VITA (Volunteer Income Tax Assistance) provides free preparation at community sites, IRS Free File works for incomes under $84,000, and MyFreeTaxes.com is free for simple returns. The EITC is worth up to $7,830 with 3+ children. You can file late returns for the past 3 years and still get refunds.
action: If you haven't filed, start at myfreetaxes.com or find a VITA site at irs.treasury.gov/freetaxprep. You may be owed thousands.
author: Prof. Nino — 60608
title_es: Qué Pasa Cuando No Declaras Impuestos — y Lo Que Estás Dejando
normal_es: Cada año presentas una declaración de impuestos. El IRS calcula lo que debes o lo que te deben. Para trabajadores de bajos ingresos, el reembolso puede valer miles de dólares.
broken_es: 5 millones de personas elegibles para el EITC no declaran impuestos, dejando $7 mil millones sin reclamar.
fix_es: Si ganaste cualquier ingreso — incluso a tiempo parcial — declara. VITA provee preparación gratis. MyFreeTaxes.com es gratis para declaraciones simples.
action_es: Si no has declarado, empieza en myfreetaxes.com o encuentra un sitio VITA. Pueden deberte miles.
